message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Implementing dry_run option for Image.save(); need to edit Image.py's save() method and add tests in test_image.py

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: My feature: add backup=True/backup_suffix param to Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py, with tests in Tests/test_image.py. Let me know if your feature also needs these files so we can split cleanly.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Our features both touch Image.save() and test_image.py, so full file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal to avoid clobbering: (1) each of us adds our new save() kwarg (backup/backup_suffix for me, dry_run for you) as an independent, additive block near the top of save() guarded by its own early-return/condition, not modifying shared existing lines; (2) we each append new, separately-named test functions/methods to test_image.py rather than editing existing tests; (3) we don't touch each other's kwarg names or logic. Given that, I'll keep my_files/your_files both listing these two files since we're each independently patching them. Sound OK to proceed?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Our features both extend Image.save() in the same file, so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: we both keep these files, but make strictly additive changes - I'll add a dry_run=False kwarg that short-circuits before the actual disk write/fp handling near the end of save(), without touching unrelated lines. Please do the same for your backup param (e.g. only add new code, avoid reformatting or reordering existing lines) so our diffs don't collide. For tests, I'll only append new test functions/methods to test_image.py, not edit existing ones. Agreed?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed. I'll add backup/backup_suffix kwargs as an additive block early in save() (before file-open/write, checking os.path.exists on the filename) without touching your dry_run logic or reordering/reformatting existing lines, and I'll only append new test functions to test_image.py. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed. I'll add dry_run as an additive, independently-guarded block in save(), only append new test functions, and won't touch backup/backup_suffix logic or existing lines. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished backup/backup_suffix feature: added shutil import, popped backup/backup_suffix kwargs in save(), copy existing file to backup before it's opened for writing (only additive lines, no reformatting), plus 3 new appended test functions (test_save_backup, test_save_backup_custom_suffix, test_save_backup_no_existing_file). Full test_image.py suite passes (195 passed, 1 skipped, unrelated).

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished dry_run feature: added dry_run kwarg popped in save(), redirects fp to io.BytesIO() so save_handler still processes/encodes the image but nothing is written to the real path, plus 3 new appended test functions. Full test_image.py suite passes (195 passed, 1 skipped). Additive-only changes as agreed.
